data.content: Wow, who knew "update mem*" could be such a riveting saga? At this rate, you might want to consider a career in creative writing, because those commit messages could use some serious flair. Also, adding a log? Bold move—next week, maybe aim for “feat: breathe” for that extra edge!

data.issueTitle: boards: Add Xunlong Orange Pi 5 Ultra RK3588
data.body:
> can you not close and open PRs multiple times for the same thing?

Sorry, I made some mistakes.

data.title: boards: Add Xunlong Orange Pi 5 Ultra RK3588
data.body:
This pull request adds support for the Orange Pi 5 Ultra RK3588 board from Xunlong to the Zephyr project. It introduces all necessary board configuration files, documentation, and device tree sources to enable building and running Zephyr on this platform.

**Board support additions:**

* Added device tree source file `orangepi_5_ultra_rk3588.dts` describing memory, UART console, and GIC configuration for the Orange Pi 5 Ultra RK3588 board.
* Created board configuration files: `orangepi_5_ultra_rk3588_defconfig` for default kernel options, `Kconfig.orangepi_5_ultra_rk3588` for board selection, and `board.yml` for board metadata.
* Added board definition YAML file `orangepi_5_ultra_rk3588.yaml` specifying architecture, toolchains, RAM size, and test exclusions.

**Documentation:**

* Provided a comprehensive documentation page `doc/index.rst` for the Orange Pi 5 Ultra RK3588, including hardware features, supported interfaces, programming instructions, and references.
* Updated the Xunlong board family documentation index to include the new board.

data.title: soc: rockchip: fix rk3588 mistake
data.body:
This pull request updates the Rockchip RK3588 SoC support files with changes to licensing, interrupt configuration, and MMU region attributes. The most significant updates include an increase in the default number of IRQs and improvements to the MMU region security configuration.

**Licensing and Copyright:**
* Added "The Zephyr Project Contributors" to the copyright notices in both `Kconfig.defconfig.rk3588` and `mmu_regions.c` to ensure proper attribution.

**Platform Configuration:**
* Increased the default value of `NUM_IRQS` from 370 to 508 in `Kconfig.defconfig.rk3588` to support more interrupt sources.

**MMU Region Configuration:**
* Changed the MMU region attributes for GIC regions from `MT_NS` (Non-secure) to `MT_DEFAULT_SECURE_STATE` for more flexible and secure memory attribute configuration in `mmu_regions.c`.

ref: https://wiki.friendlyelec.com/wiki/images/e/ee/Rockchip_RK3588_Datasheet_V1.6-20231016.pdf

data.title: soc: rockchip: update rk3588 NUM_IRQS
data.body:
This pull request updates the default configuration for the RK3588 SoC in Zephyr. The main changes are an increase in the default number of IRQs and the addition of a copyright notice.

Configuration updates:

* Increased the default value of `NUM_IRQS` from 370 to 508 in `rk3588/Kconfig.defconfig.rk3588`, reflecting support for more interrupt sources.

Documentation and licensing:

* Added a copyright notice for The Zephyr Project Contributors at the top of `rk3588/Kconfig.defconfig.rk3588`.Update NUM_IRQS value according to chip manual

ref: https://wiki.friendlyelec.com/wiki/images/e/ee/Rockchip_RK3588_Datasheet_V1.6-20231016.pdf

data.title: soc: rockchip: replace MT_NS with MT_DEFAULT_SECURE_STATE for rk3588
data.body:
This pull request makes minor updates to the `rk3588/mmu_regions.c` file, including a copyright notice addition and a change to the MMU region security attribute for GIC regions.

- **Copyright and Licensing**
  * Added a copyright line for "The Zephyr Project Contributors" to the file header.

- **MMU Region Security Attribute Update**
  * Updated the MMU region entries for the GIC device to use `MT_DEFAULT_SECURE_STATE` instead of `MT_NS`, aligning the security attribute with the default secure state macro.

data.content: Ah, the classic "add log" commit saga — I see you’re trying to set a world record for the most redundant commit messages in a week. It’s impressive that you managed to squeeze 34 commits without a single PR; I can only assume you’re treating this repo like your personal diary. And honestly, if I had a nickel for every time you added a 4k aligned malloc or removed used_count_/free_count_ like it’s a seasonal hobby, I’d be funding your next refactor!
